verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70-1/HSP72) is canonically a cytosolic stress-inducible chaperone with no TM domain, GPI anchor, or classical secretory signal. However, multiple independent studies document its translocation to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane specifically in tumor cells and under stress conditions (heat, oxidative stress, cancer transformation). HSP70/HSPA1A is identified on the cell surface of tumor cells via lipid raft-mediated, phosphatidylserine-dependent membrane insertion. NK cells target membrane-HSP70 on tumor cells, and multiple therapeutic programs exploit this ecto-form. Surface display is stress/cancer-state-induced and not a baseline cytosolic-cell property, qualifying as contextual/cell_state_induced.
